corpus.title: 'I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation': Trump says on Iran war
corpus.signal_text:
'I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation': Trump says on Iran war

President Trump stated that American financial struggles will not influence his Iran negotiations, emphasizing that preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon is his sole priority. White House Communications Director Steven Cheung affirmed that Trump's ultimate responsibility is American safety and security, as Iran acquiring a nuclear weapon would threaten all Americans.

corpus.title: US President Trump And China's Xi Set For Talks Spanning Iran, Nuclear, Trade And AI From May 13-15
corpus.signal_text:
US President Trump And China's Xi Set For Talks Spanning Iran, Nuclear, Trade And AI From May 13-15

U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping will discuss critical issues like Iran, Taiwan, AI, and nuclear weapons during Trump’s two-day visit to China, marking their first in-person talks in over six months. They aim to stabilize strained ties amid previous trade disputes.

corpus.title: US and Israeli strikes hit Iran sites tied to nuclear weapon work, think tank says
corpus.signal_text:
US and Israeli strikes hit Iran sites tied to nuclear weapon work, think tank says

At least six Iranian nuclear sites were attacked in recent US and Israeli strikes, with most confirmed or suspected targets tied to work needed to build a nuclear weapon, a new satellite-imagery analysis by the Institute for Science and International Security shows.

corpus.title: Trump: 'I don't think about Americans’ financial situation'
corpus.signal_text:
This is an entry from: Live: China urges Pakistan to intensify mediation ahead of Trump visit
Trump: 'I don't think about Americans’ financial situation'
13 May 2026 10:09 BST
US President Donald Trump has said the financial situation of Americans doesn't factor into his thinking with regards to Iran negotiations.
Speaking in response to a question from a reporter outside the White House, Trump said the only thing that mattered to him was preventing Iran from developing a nuclear weapon.
"Not even a little bit - the only thing that matters when I'm talking about Iran, they can't have a nuclear weapon," he said.
"I don't think about Americans' financial situation, I don't think about anybody."
Iran has long denied it is seeking a nuclear weapon.
